title: UK Government Announces Social Media Ban for Children Under 16
subtitle: New rules aim to protect young people from online harm by spring 2027.

summary.en: The UK government has announced a plan to ban children under 16 from major social media platforms by early 2027. Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated the law is necessary to protect young people from bullying and mental health issues. Major tech companies like Meta and YouTube have criticized the move, arguing it could push children toward less safe, unregulated online spaces. Messaging apps and some educational services will remain available to younger users. The government also plans to introduce new restrictions on AI chatbots and features like livestreaming for teenagers.
summary.zh: 英國政府宣布一項計畫，預計在二〇二七年初前禁止十六歲以下兒童使用主要社群媒體平台。首相施凱爾表示，此法律對於保護青少年免受霸凌與心理健康問題至關重要。Meta 與 YouTube 等大型科技公司對此提出批評，認為這可能將兒童推向安全性較低、未受監管的網路空間。通訊軟體與部分教育服務將維持開放供年輕使用者使用。政府也計畫針對青少年引入對人工智慧聊天機器人及直播等功能的新限制。

paragraphs.en: The UK government has officially announced a plan to ban children under the age of 16 from using major social media platforms. Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer confirmed that this new policy is expected to take effect in the spring of 2027. The government hopes to pass the necessary regulations before the end of this year. This decision marks a significant change in how the UK manages the online safety of young people.
paragraphs.zh: 英國政府正式宣布一項計畫，將禁止十六歲以下的兒童使用主要的社群媒體平台。英國首相施凱爾（Keir Starmer）證實，這項新政策預計於二〇二七年春季生效。政府希望在今年年底前通過必要的法規。這項決定標誌著英國在管理青少年網路安全方面出現了重大轉變。

paragraphs.en: According to the BBC, the ban will apply to popular apps including TikTok, Snapchat, Instagram, Facebook, and X. However, not all online services will be affected. Messaging apps like WhatsApp and Signal will remain available, and the educational platform YouTube Kids is also exempt from the new rules. The government noted that it is still finalizing the full list of platforms that will be included in the ban.
paragraphs.zh: 根據英國廣播公司（BBC）報導，這項禁令將適用於包括 TikTok、Snapchat、Instagram、Facebook 和 X 在內的熱門應用程式。然而，並非所有網路服務都會受到影響。像 WhatsApp 和 Signal 這類通訊軟體將維持開放，而教育平台 YouTube Kids 也不在新規範的限制範圍內。政府指出，目前仍在敲定受禁令影響的平台完整清單。

paragraphs.en: Prime Minister Starmer explained that the government is taking this step because social media can be harmful to children. He stated that these platforms can make children unhappy and make it easier for bullies to harass them. "I am not prepared to compromise on the safety and happiness of our children," Sir Keir said during the announcement. He argued that while social media has some benefits, the government must prioritize the protection of young people.
paragraphs.zh: 施凱爾首相解釋說，政府採取這項行動是因為社群媒體可能對兒童造成傷害。他表示，這些平台可能導致兒童不快樂，並讓霸凌者更容易騷擾他們。施凱爾在宣布時表示：「我不會在我們孩子的安全與快樂上妥協。」他主張，儘管社群媒體具備某些益處，但政府必須優先考量對青少年的保護。

paragraphs.en: Technology Secretary Liz Kendall spoke in the House of Commons, noting that the measures are designed to take power away from large tech companies. She stated that these businesses have had many chances to keep children safe but have not done enough. The government also plans to introduce other restrictions for those under 18, such as banning intimate or sexual chats with AI chatbots. Additionally, officials are looking into potential curfews and limits on "infinite scrolling"—a feature that keeps users on an app for a long time—for 16 and 17-year-olds.
paragraphs.zh: 科技大臣肯達爾（Liz Kendall）在下議院發言時指出，這些措施旨在削弱大型科技公司的權力。她表示，這些企業已有多次機會確保兒童安全，但做得還不夠。政府也計畫針對十八歲以下族群引入其他限制，例如禁止與人工智慧聊天機器人進行親密或性方面的對話。此外，官員們正在研擬針對十六與十七歲青少年實施宵禁，並限制「無限捲動」功能，這項功能會讓使用者長時間停留在應用程式上。

paragraphs.en: Major technology companies have expressed strong disagreement with the plan. The Guardian reports that Meta, which owns Facebook and Instagram, warned that a ban could isolate teenagers from their online communities. Meta suggested that such rules might drive young people toward unregulated platforms that lack any parental controls or safety features. Similarly, YouTube stated that blanket bans push children out of supervised, beneficial environments and toward services that are less safe.
paragraphs.zh: 大型科技公司對此計畫表達了強烈反對。衛報（The Guardian）報導，擁有 Facebook 和 Instagram 的 Meta 公司警告，禁令可能會使青少年與其網路社群隔絕。Meta 認為，這類規定可能會將年輕人推向缺乏任何家長監護或安全功能的未受監管平台。同樣地，YouTube 也表示，全面禁止會將兒童從受到監督且有益的環境中推向安全性較低的服務。

paragraphs.en: Snapchat also criticized the move, arguing that because most of its users connect with friends and family, an outright ban would not necessarily make them safer. Instead, the company suggested that it might simply force teenagers to use other, less secure services. TikTok responded by saying it would examine the details of the government's measures and look forward to working with officials on the issue.
paragraphs.zh: Snapchat 也批評此舉，認為由於其大多數使用者是與親友聯繫，全面禁止並不一定能讓他們更安全。相反地，該公司認為這可能只是迫使青少年使用其他安全性較差的服務。TikTok 則回應表示，將會審視政府措施的細節，並期待就此議題與官員合作。

paragraphs.en: Despite the criticism from tech firms, the government’s plan has received support from various groups, including parents who have lost children to online harms. Esther Ghey, whose daughter Brianna was killed, welcomed the announcement. She told the BBC that she believes the ban will help save many children's lives. Joe Ryrie, a co-founder of the Smartphone Free Childhood campaign, also supported the decision, calling it a major step forward that will give children more time to grow up before entering complex online environments.
paragraphs.zh: 儘管面臨科技公司的批評，政府的計畫仍獲得各界支持，包括那些因網路傷害而失去孩子的家長。女兒布莉安娜（Brianna）不幸遇害的艾絲特·蓋伊（Esther Ghey）對此宣布表示歡迎。她告訴 BBC，她相信這項禁令將有助於挽救許多孩子的生命。「無智慧型手機童年」（Smartphone Free Childhood）運動的共同創辦人喬·萊里（Joe Ryrie）也支持這項決定，稱其為向前邁出的重要一步，將讓孩子在進入複雜的網路環境前，有更多時間成長。

paragraphs.en: To enforce these rules, the government said that platforms will be required to verify the ages of their users. While the exact system for age checks is still being decided, the government suggested that many adults might not need to do anything if their accounts are already linked to credit cards or verified email addresses. The government also noted that the ban is part of an international trend, as other countries have recently introduced similar restrictions to protect young people online.
paragraphs.zh: 為了執行這些規定，政府表示平台將被要求驗證使用者的年齡。雖然年齡查核的確切系統仍在研議中，但政府暗示，如果成年人的帳戶已連結信用卡或經過驗證的電子郵件地址，他們可能無需進行額外操作。政府也指出，這項禁令是國際趨勢的一部分，因為其他國家近期也引入了類似的限制，以保護網路上的年輕人。

paragraphs.en: As the UK moves toward the 2027 deadline, the government has made it clear that this is not the end of their work. Technology Secretary Liz Kendall emphasized that the announcement is not a "one and done" event. She stated that there is still much more to do to ensure that both children and adults remain safe while using social media. For now, the government continues to consult with experts and the public to shape the final details of these new, world-leading protections.
paragraphs.zh: 隨著英國邁向二〇二七年的期限，政府明確表示這並非工作的終點。科技大臣肯達爾強調，這項宣布並非「一次性」的行動。她表示，為了確保兒童與成人在使用社群媒體時都能保持安全，還有許多工作要做。目前，政府持續與專家及大眾進行諮詢，以制定這些世界領先的新保護措施之最終細節。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To become active or begin to apply (usually referring to a law, rule, or policy).
keyTerms.definitionZh: （法律、規定或政策）開始生效、實施。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個常見的法律與政策用語，容易被誤解為「採取行動」。文中：Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer confirmed that this new policy is expected to take effect in the spring of 2027.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To lower one's standards or principles, or to put something at risk.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 妥協、讓步；危及（原則或安全）。
keyTerms.noteZh: 在日常生活中常指「折衷方案」，但在這裡指「在原則上讓步」。文中："I am not prepared to compromise on the safety and happiness of our children," Sir Keir said during the announcement.

keyTerms.definitionEn: Something that happens only once and is then finished, with no further action required.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 一次性的、做完就結束的事。
keyTerms.noteZh: 這是一個口語化的片語，形容某事並非「一次性解決就沒事了」。文中：Technology Secretary Liz Kendall emphasized that the announcement is not a "one and done" event.

sources.title: Under-16s will be banned from social media from early 2027

sources.title: Social media firms hit back as Starmer announces ban for under-16s in UK

sources.title: UK to ban social media for kids under 16, may impose overnight curfews

previousArticles.title: UK Government Announces Social Media Ban for Under-16s
previousArticles.summaryEn: The UK government has announced a plan to ban children under 16 from using major social media platforms. Prime Minister Keir Starmer stated that the law is necessary to protect young people from online harm and bullying. While the ban will not include messaging apps, it will restrict features like livestreaming and contact with strangers. Major technology companies have expressed concern that the ban could push children toward less safe online spaces. The new rules are expected to come into effect in the spring of 2027.

previousArticles.title: Anthropic Disables Advanced AI Models Following US Government Order
previousArticles.summaryEn: Anthropic has decided to turn off its most powerful AI models, Fable 5 and Mythos 5. This follows a direct order from the US government to block access for foreign nationals. The government claims there are national security risks related to how the models could be used. Anthropic disagrees with the decision and says the government provided little evidence. The move highlights growing tension between AI companies and government regulators.

quiz.question: Which of the following services will be permitted for children under 16 according to the proposed UK government regulations?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，政府明確指出 WhatsApp 等通訊軟體以及 YouTube Kids 教育平台將不受禁令影響。選項 A、C 與 D 皆屬於文章中明確提到將被禁止或受到限制的平台與功能，因此皆為錯誤選項。

quiz.question: What is a primary concern shared by major tech companies like Meta and Snapchat regarding the proposed ban?

options.A: The financial loss caused by losing younger users' subscription fees.
options.B: The technical difficulty of verifying the ages of millions of users.
options.C: The risk that teenagers will migrate to less secure, unregulated online spaces.
options.D: The lack of consultation between the government and private technology firms.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章第五段與第六段，Meta 與 Snapchat 皆表達了類似擔憂：禁令可能導致青少年轉向缺乏家長監護或安全機制的平台。選項 A 文章未提及；選項 B 雖是執行層面的挑戰，但非科技公司反對的主因；選項 D 則與文中提到政府正與專家及相關單位諮詢的內容不符。

quiz.question: In the fourth paragraph, what does the phrase 'one and done' imply about the government's approach to online safety?

options.A: The government believes this single policy will solve all online problems.
options.B: The government views this policy as just one part of an ongoing effort.
options.C: The government is hesitant to implement further rules after this ban.
options.D: The government intends to complete all safety measures in a single attempt.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文中提到這不是一個 'one and done' 的事件，結合上下文 Liz Kendall 的發言，意指這項政策並非一次性解決所有問題，而是持續努力的一部分。選項 A 與 D 剛好與原文意涵相反；選項 C 則誤解了政府持續推動安全措施的積極態度。

quiz.question: What is the central message of the article regarding the UK's new social media policy?

options.A: The UK government is banning all internet usage for children under 16 to ensure their academic success.
options.B: Tech companies are successfully blocking the government's attempt to regulate social media.
options.C: The government is implementing a controversial but prioritized ban on social media for children to improve their safety.
options.D: Parents are the main force behind the development of new AI-driven age verification technology.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。文章主旨圍繞在英國政府計畫禁止 16 歲以下兒童使用社交媒體，以保護其身心安全，儘管面臨科技公司的強烈反對，但仍獲得部分家長團體支持。選項 A 過度誇大；選項 B 與事實不符，政府並未停止計畫；選項 D 則錯誤地將家長的角色歸因於技術開發，而非政策支持。
